name: FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) Flows — India
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday): **THE DII BUFFER HELD THE FLOOR ON CONFIRMED FII SELLING — BUT THE DEPLOYMENT SUFFICIENT CONDITION (FII NET-POSITIVE 3+ CONSECUTIVE SESSIONS) REMAINS UNMET: BOTH OBSERVED SESSIONS WERE NEGATIVE.** (intel A7 / markets) Three-day window (June 7 cutoff -> early June 10; June 8/9 runs empty). The two observed prints: FII net **-Rs5,556cr** (Mon 8 Jun) and ~**-Rs4,566cr** (Tue 9 Jun); DII net **+Rs5,165cr** (Mon) absorbed **~93%** of the FII selling — the cleanest single-session demonstration this cycle that the DII buffer, not foreign flow, is the proximate support holding Nifty's 23,000 floor (Mon closed 23,123 above the ~23,091 GIFT-indicated gap-down, then the index rose Tue/Wed on Trump's tone). The capital_substitution-reversal thesis is therefore NEGATIVELY tested, not advanced: the AI trade (leg one) cracked but India FII is STILL net-selling (leg two unmet). The FII-exhaustion/H2-reversal thesis (Outlook Business et al. — ownership below DII, ~90% absorption, 'experts see an H2 reversal') is being recycled VERBATIM across outlets — [UNCHANGED], NOT a new development; no genuinely new exhaustion datum landed this window. **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: 'FII net-positive 3+/5+ sessions' STAYS WATCHING — UNMET, both observed sessions negative; the 'Rs2.5L cr outflow / DII-channel-cracks' trigger STAYS WATCHING — the outflow limb is long since met but the DII channel did NOT crack (it absorbed ~93% and held the floor). PRIOR — 2026-06-07 (Sunday): **FII OWNERSHIP OF INDIAN EQUITIES HAS FALLEN TO A ~TWO-DECADE LOW (~14.7%, APRIL) AND NOW SITS *BELOW* DII OWNERSHIP (~18.9%) FOR THE FIRST TIME IN THE MODERN HISTORY OF INDIAN CAPITAL MARKETS — A STRUCTURAL MILESTONE.** (intel A4, CONFIRMED/REPORTED) 2026 YTD FII net selling is in the ~Rs2.5-3.0 lakh crore range (sources vary: ~Rs2.3L cr Jan-May, ~Rs2.54L cr cited 5 Jun, up to ~Rs2.99L cr on one tally — all already exceeding 2025's full-year ~Rs1.7-2.4L cr record), with DIIs absorbing ~90% (May DII net buying ~Rs82,600 cr). A NEW analyst camp has emerged arguing FII selling is NEARING EXHAUSTION (ownership below DII; ~90% DII absorption; 'experts see an H2 2026 reversal') — the bullish counter to the Kotak 'India-stays-muted' read: the marginal foreign seller is running out of stock, so further FII downside is structurally limited. FII was STILL net-selling through Friday's Asia session; Monday 8 June (India's first reaction to the US rout) is the real flow test. **The deployment-thesis sufficient condition — FII net-positive 3+ sessions — remains UNMET and untested.** Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange). **THE SINK CRACKED HARD — BUT FII IS STILL NET-SELLING, AND THE YTD OUTFLOW NOW EXCEEDS THE ENTIRE 2025 RECORD WITH THE FOREIGN BET AT A DECADE LOW.** Two data points define the channel today. (1) THE OUTFLOW IS NOW A RECORD ON A NEW MEASURE: FIIs have withdrawn ~Rs2.54 lakh crore from Indian secondary equities YTD 2026, already EXCEEDING the ~Rs2.4 lakh crore sold in ALL of 2025 (itself a record) — 'India's foreign-investor bet has shrunk to a DECADE LOW as the AI trade draws capital away' (Outlook Business). DIIs are absorbing ~90% of the FII selling; the DII buffer is now THE transmission mechanism keeping Nifty rangebound (markets E4). (2) THE SINK CRACKED HARD: the crowded AI/semi trade that has been the destination for capital fleeing India took its biggest crack yet on the 5-June US session — Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + a hot May jobs print (172K vs ~85K) drove SMH -9.2%, Nasdaq -4%, VIX +39.7% to ~21.5. For 4+ weeks this node held that India's re-rating requires the FII flow to TURN, which requires winning capital back from the AI trade OR that trade cracking — it has now cracked. BUT the capital_substitution thesis has NOT confirmed LEG TWO: FII is still net-selling, and the India-rotation implication is genuinely two-camp ANALYST OPINION — Kotak Institutional Equities says FPI flows STAY MUTED (India has limited AI-supply-chain exposure, faces its own drags, 'low attractiveness vs other EMs'; needs an idiosyncratic catalyst), while Morgan Stanley GIC says rotate AI 'builders' -> 'adopters' and add EM/rest-of-world. India also closed BEFORE the rout (Monday 8 June is the real test), and a violent (non-orderly) AI crack is first a global risk-off that pulls capital OUT of EM India before any rotation-back. The 4-June WATCH trigger ('North-Asia AI corrects >10% from peak AND India FII net-positive 3+ sessions') has advanced on LEG ONE (the correction) but NOT leg two (the flow turn) — stays WATCHING. SYNTHESIS: necessary condition (AI crack) partially met; sufficient condition (capital choosing India) NOT demonstrated. DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor until the FII flow confirms a turn. PRIOR — Day 97 (Thursday June 4; covers 3 June afternoon -> 4 June US day / 5 June IST). **THE OUTFLOW STAYED HEAVY BUT DID NOT SET A NEW RECORD — AND THE SINK THE CAPITAL HAS BEEN FLOWING INTO TOOK ITS FIRST CRACK.** Two updates reframe the FII channel constructively for the first time in weeks: (1) the 3 June FII outflow (~Rs5,600cr) was heavy but BELOW the 2 June record (Rs8,362.92cr) — the record was a one-session spike, not a new run-rate, so the outflow is not freshly accelerating; (2) decisively, the North-Asia AI-memory supercycle that has been the SINK for FII fleeing India (the nifty-50 <-> semiconductors capital_substitution edge) took its FIRST valuation-driven crack — KOSPI -5.3%, Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9% on Broadcom's guidance miss (markets C1/E1). For 4+ weeks this node has held that India's re-rating requires the FII flow to TURN, which requires winning capital back from the North-Asia AI trade (or that trade cracking). Today the trade cracked. IF Broadcom marks the top of the AI-capex-justification trade, the capital_substitution edge can fire in REVERSE (AI-memory sell -> EM/India bid) — the cleanest forward catalyst for the India deployment thesis. A WATCH trigger was added (markets E1): North-Asia AI corrects >10% from peak AND India FII turns net-positive for 3+ sessions = the substitution reverses. **NOT YET A CONFIRMED TURN**: one session is not a reversal; the binding reversibility condition — FII flow positive for 5 consecutive sessions — is still unconfirmed, and a VIOLENT (non-orderly) AI crack is first a global risk-off that pulls capital OUT of EM India too before any rotation-back. The other reversibility conditions held this window: Brent <$95 (oil softening) and the rupee firmed sub-Rs96; RBI held 5.25% (neutral). DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor until the FII flow confirms a turn. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers 2 June afternoon -> 3 June morning). **THE FII CHANNEL HIT A NEW EXTREME — A RECORD SINGLE-DAY OUTFLOW (Rs8,362.92cr, 2 June) AND A RECORD ~$26.8bn YTD NET OUTFLOW, NOW COMPOUNDED BY A US-TARIFF VECTOR.** The binding constraint on India deepened: FIIs sold a net Rs8,362.92cr on 2 June (largest single-day outflow in recent sessions, The Week), taking 2026 cumulative net outflow to a record ~$26.8bn. The structural read is unchanged and reinforced — foreign capital is rotating OUT of expensive India (~24x P/E) INTO the North-Asia AI-memory supercycle (KOSPI +31.5% MoM, Taiwan +11.9% MoM, SMH +26.1% MoM). NEW: a USTR-proposed 12.5% Section 301 tariff on India adds a non-oil, non-crisis headwind that does NOT reverse on a Hormuz deal — the first such structural India headwind to enter the graph (markets E1). India gets the oil HEADWIND on re-escalation, a TARIFF headwind, and continued FII flight, with NO relief on de-escalation because the marginal seller is structurally exiting for AI. DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn: (a) Brent <$95 sustained + (b) FII flow positive + (c) organic USD/INR Rs93-94 — and the tariff + record outflow pushed the setup further away. PRIOR — Day 95 (Monday June 2; covers 30 May afternoon -> 2 June morning). **THE FII CHANNEL IS WHY INDIA'S RELIEF LEG FAILS IN BOTH DIRECTIONS — AND THE OIL-UP MOVE CONFIRMED IT AGAIN. Markets reopened: Nifty fell a 4th straight session (-0.70%, 1 June) as the +7% Brent gap revived the import-bill headwind AND foreign-investor outflows continued (FY26 ~Rs3.33 lakh cr). The structural read holds: foreign capital is rotating OUT of expensive India (~24x P/E) INTO the North-Asia AI-memory supercycle — the SAME Taiwan +16.5% / Nikkei +10.6% / SMH +22.9% trade that lifted US records on 1 June. India gets the oil HEADWIND on re-escalation but NOT the relief on de-escalation precisely because the binding marginal seller is structurally exiting for AI, not the oil bill. The nifty-50 <-> semiconductors capital-substitution edge re-confirmed (markets E3) — the cleanest explanation of the failure. DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn: (a) Brent <$95 sustained + (b) FII flow positive + (c) organic USD/INR Rs93-94 — and the oil-up move pushed (a) and (c) the wrong way. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **FII/PASSIVE FLOWS WERE THE TRANSMISSION MECHANISM IN EVERY INDIA CHAIN AGAIN — NOW WITH A NEW MECHANICAL LEG. The 29 May Nifty -1.5% drop on a crisis-RELIEF day was driven by (1) the MSCI Global Standard Index rebalance taking effect at the close -> an estimated $800m-$1bn of foreign PASSIVE outflows (Nuvama via wire; Hyundai India -$281m, Jubilant -$161m the steepest) — one-time, mechanical, calendar-driven, non-recurring; (2) discretionary FII selling; (3) structural rotation OUT of expensive India (~24x P/E) INTO the Korea/Taiwan AI-memory supercycle (India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%; Goldman's paired call: India marketweight / Korea highest-conviction +300% EPS). Cumulative war-period outflows >$22bn (stocks+bonds); FY26 net FPI ~-$16.7bn. **This is the node that explains why India's relief leg fails: even with every crisis variable moving India's way, global capital reallocation to AI overwhelmed it.** The new nifty-50 <-> semiconductors capital-substitution edge formalizes the channel (markets E1).** Created 2026-05-12. **FY26 FII OUTFLOWS NOW ~₹3.33 LAKH CRORE — THE CAPITAL IS ROTATING OUT OF INDIA INTO THE KOREA/TAIWAN AI TRADE, AND OIL RELIEF CAN'T REVERSE IT.** Day 90 (Thursday May 28): the FII channel — THE transmission mechanism in every India chain — stayed the binding negative. FY26 cumulative outflows are now ~₹3.33 lakh crore (Republic World May 28; the running tally has climbed from the ~₹1.92 lakh cr CY26 figure carried last brief), against a 15-year-low FPI ownership (~16%, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). The decisive read: this is not merely crisis-driven de-risking — foreign money is rotating OUT of India INTO the Korea/Taiwan AI trade (India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%), so ~40% of the India weakness is a valuation-rotation that does NOT reverse on a deal, while ~60% is the crisis component that does. **Why oil relief can't fix it**: India printed a second straight down session despite operative Brent sub-$100 — oil-import relief is necessary but not sufficient when the marginal seller is structurally exiting an expensive (~24x P/E), FII-dependent market for a cheaper, faster-growing AI-semis EM. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a) but (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed and FY26 outflows are still climbing. DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. PRIOR (May 27): **THE RELIEF SESSIONS DID NOT TURN THE FII CHANNEL — THE BOUNCE WAS PAPER, NOT POSITIONING, AND IT REVERSED.** FII flow is THE transmission mechanism in every India causal chain of the cycle, and over the ~48h window (no brief May 26) it stayed the binding negative: record FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore YTD 2026 (Republic World; already exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-yr low, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). The May 25 relief bounce (Nifty +1.32%) reversed when US CONFIRMED strikes inside southern Iran 'dampened hopes for a peace deal' — India gave back the rally May 26 (Nifty -0.49%) as FII outflows weighed and only DII inflows cushioned (the Nifty MidCap still printed a fresh high on DII absorption). **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: oil-relief + the RBI $5B swap (~1.96x cover, healthy dollar appetite) + the 'undervalued' jawboning can cap the currency and bid the tape, but they cannot make FIIs net buyers — relief is paper, positioning is structural. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a) but FII and INR both moved the WRONG way this window; (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed. The markets dossier promotes fii-flows as the recurring transmission mechanism (E4) and is explicit that the crisis component reverses fast on a SIGNED deal while the FII/structural component does not. PRIOR (May 25): **MAY 25 WAS A DEAL-RELIEF SESSION (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22, India VIX -7.9%) BUT THE STRUCTURAL FII OVERHANG IS UNCHANGED.** With US/UK shut for holiday, India's domestic-only session bid the deal-relief leg (Brent <$100 + US-Iran de-escalation hopes + RBI 'INR undervalued' jawboning) — yet the FII channel did NOT structurally turn: YTD CY26 outflow still tracking >₹2.19 lakh crore (already exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII first time ever / DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500), and DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. **The relief bounce is paper, not positioning** — a single risk-on session lifts the index but does not reverse the structural rotation out of Indian equities; the markets dossier is explicit that ~75% of today's Nifty gain reverses on deal-failure + oil re-spike. The FOURTH fuel hike (₹2.61-2.71/L May 25, cumulative ~₹7.5/L) compresses RBI optionality and reinforces the FII-deterrence backdrop. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Monday's relief session moved (a) and (c) marginally in the right direction but (b) — the binding condition — is unconfirmed. Tuesday May 26 ($5B RBI swap auction + Quad) is the next institutional read. PRIOR (May 24): **MAY MTD NET SALES ~₹25,896 CR PER GROWW/NSE (PACE FLAT VS PRIOR BRIEF). FII YTD CY26 OUTFLOW TRACKING > ₹2.19 LAKH CRORE PER PRIOR BRIEF. FPI OWNERSHIP ~16% (15-YEAR LOW, BELOW DII FIRST TIME EVER). FRIDAY MAY 22 DII +₹6,003 CR ABSORBED FII -₹4,440 CR — DOMESTIC INSTITUTIONS ABSORBING FOREIGN OUTFLOWS. THE FRIDAY DII/FII SPLIT IS THE CRITICAL SIGNAL: DOMESTIC BUYING IS THE ONLY FLOOR. IF DII BID FADES (MUTUAL FUND SIP FLOW DECLINES), NIFTY 21,500 (200-DEMA) IS THE NEXT TEST.** Monday May 25 first FII print of the Trump 'no rush' Sunday pivot is the operational test. **The non-mover Indian equities Friday despite Brent -5% weekly + INR strengthening + global AI rally is the cleanest demonstration that FII channel is held by structural sell-flow that does NOT reverse on intervention-driven INR retracement.** Reversibility requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR retracement to ₹93 (organic) — none imminent. PRIOR (May 23): MAY MTD NET SALES ~₹25,896 CR PER GROWW/NSE (PACE FLAT VS MAY 22 BRIEF). DII ₹6,003 CR ABSORPTION FRIDAY. CAPITAL-FLOW DIRECTION = STRUCTURAL ROTATION OUT OF INDIAN EQUITIES CONTINUES EVEN AS CYCLE DE-ESCALATES RHETORICALLY.** USD/INR ₹95.68 May 22 close (RBI ~$5B intervention drove rupee strengthening). **The non-mover Nifty 23,719.30 +0.3% 1D across the deal-anticipation window despite Brent -1.5% MoM + INR strengthening is the cleanest demonstration that FII channel is held by structural sell-flow that does NOT reverse on intervention-driven INR retracement.** FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII first time ever) unchanged. YTD CY26 FII outflow tracking ~₹2 lakh cr. **The Friday tape's FII-DII divergence**: equity (DII-buyers) vs FX (FII-outflows) is the cleanest India structural signal of cycle. **Saturday May 23 third petrol/diesel hike (cumulative ₹4.74 petrol)** is politically inflammatory pass-through that compresses RBI policy optionality and feeds back into FII deterrence. **Reversibility requires**: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR retracement to ₹93 (organic, not intervention-driven). None imminent. **Sonal Varma (Nomura)**: structural AI-disruption-to-India-IT-services concerns + RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging increased FX hedge costs deterring portfolio inflows; sees further INR depreciation scope. PRIOR (May 22): **MAY MTD NET SALES ₹25,896 CRORE PER GROWW/NSE PRIMARY (BSE SECONDARY) — PACE FLAT VERSUS THE MAY 19 BRIEF'S ₹27,048 CRORE READING. THE STRUCTURAL ROTATION CONTINUES WITHOUT ACCELERATION; INR INTERVENTION-DRIVEN RETRACEMENT TO ₹95.69 DOES NOT REVERSE THE UNDERLYING FLOW.** May 20 alone: net sales -₹1,597 crore. **Camp B framing nuance** (per intel A3): on the kinetic axis, INR retraced (geopolitical relief read), but FII outflows continued, reserves bled, and the underlying structural bear holds. **The reversal is INTERVENTION-DRIVEN, not flow-driven**: RBI is paying ~$5B in reserves over 48 hours to defend ₹95.50-96.00, demonstrating that the rupee defense is now operationally explicit. **FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, below DII first time ever)** unchanged. **YTD CY26 FII outflow tracking ~₹2 lakh cr** vs full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr — overshoot intact. **The non-mover Sensex/Nifty across the deal-anticipation window** despite Brent -5.3% + INR +0.78% retracement is the cleanest demonstration that FII channel is held by structural sell-flow that does NOT reverse on intervention-driven INR retracement. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — that requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR retracement to ₹93 (organic, not intervention-driven). **Forex reserves $688.9B week ending May 15** (-$8B from $696.9B) confirms structural drawdown rate; sustainability ~30 weeks at current pace, ~8-12 weeks in stress acceleration. **Bank Nifty -11.8% 3M = cumulative RBI defense MTM cost** does NOT reverse on crisis resolution. **Sonal Varma (Nomura) call**: structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs deterring portfolio inflows; sees further scope for INR depreciation. **The cleanest signal of structural FII bear case validity**: even after RBI ~$5B 48hr intervention pulling INR back ₹1.46 from intra-week low, FII flows did NOT positive-pivot. Intervention can cap currency but cannot reverse foreign positioning. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **DAY 8 (May 18): ₹96 TRIGGER FIRES — USD/INR ₹96.28-96.29 RECORD LOW; FII YTD ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXIT EXCEEDS FULL-YEAR 2025 BY ~₹40K CR.** FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low, BELOW DII for first time ever). **Reuters has ranked the rupee as the worst-performing Asian currency in 2026.** Sensex/Nifty open -1.30%/-1.04% Monday on Brent $111.47 (+2.0%, StanChart $130-145 path activates), Saudi drone interception (B1), Trump-Netanyahu coordination. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing**: Brent $111 → CAD widening → FII outflow → INR ₹96 trigger fires → RBI binary (defend/capitulate/administrative). At $111 Brent, FII channel will not turn — May 14 bounce fully reversed. **Reversibility ASYMMETRIC**: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — NONE triggered, all moving wrong direction. **The RBI's weekly reserves print for week ending May 16 has NOT yet printed through Monday morning IST (lands Monday-Tuesday)**. **Tuesday May 19 Trump NSC binary**: strike order → FII channel cracks further (Nifty 21,500-22,000 test); back-off → potential FII bounce. **PRIOR (May 16): FII YTD ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXIT (EXCEEDS FULL-YEAR 2025); MAY FII RUNNING ₹14,231 CR OUTFLOW SO FAR; FPI OWNERSHIP ~16% (LOWEST IN 2 DECADES, BELOW DII FOR FIRST TIME).** **A9 RBI FOREX RESERVES — most recent data is week ending May 8 = $696.99B (+$6.3B w/w recovery from May 1 $690.69B)**; week-ending May 15 print not yet released. Yesterday's brief 'anything above $10B this week moves the sustainability question from theoretical to operational' specification remains a PENDING test. **A10 USD/INR — May 15 intraday high ₹95.9825 = 1.75 paise from breaching ₹96 Varma trigger (Day 4 of 5-7 session window)** — RBI direct intervention near ₹95.96 is the operational defence. **Sensex closed May 15 at 75,238 (-0.21%); Nifty -0.19% at 23,643.50** — Indian markets halted Thursday's bounce; late selling in metals and oil/gas; Tata Motors +5.14%, Infosys +1.92%, Tech Mahindra +1.79% (IT-pack rotation on US recession risk). Bank Nifty -0.8% / -11.9% 3M = RBI regulatory drag carries forward. **At $109 Brent, FII channel will not turn — May 14 bounce now under threat.** RBI primary-dealer trading volumes hike May 15 (annual targets for 21 bond market makers) is the liquidity-providing measure — India 10Y stayed flat-to-easing despite global yield shock. Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors (partial relief if announced). PRIOR (May 15): **DAY 5 (May 15): ₹2 LAKH CRORE EXITED INDIAN EQUITIES IN FIRST 5 MONTHS OF 2026 — SURPASSING ALL OF 2025'S ₹1.66 LAKH CRORE.** **FPI ownership now ~16% — lowest in 15-20 years AND BELOW DII FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER**. **$19B of that outflow since Iran war started — flow is overwhelmingly crisis-linked.** USD/INR ₹95.90 fourth straight record close; foreign safe-haven USD bid on Trump Iran ultimatum amplifies pressure. **Indian markets bounced May 14 after 4-session losing streak**: Sensex +789.74 / +1.06% to 75,398.72; Nifty +277 / +1.18% to 23,689.60 (pharma, healthcare and metals leading; Cipla, Adani Enterprises, Bharti Airtel top gainers) — but bounce is summit-pause-pricing on Trump-Xi joint statement, NOT regime reversal. **Government considering bond-tax cut for foreign investors — partial relief signal for INR; not yet announced.** **Sonal Varma's ₹96-within-5-7-sessions trigger Day 3 of window. RBI weekly reserves print due May 16 = next sustainability test.** PRIOR (May 14): Markets dossier C2 reversibility requirements unchanged: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — none triggered. **FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.' **DAY 4 (May 14): FII DATA VIA NSDL NOT YET RELEASED FOR MAY 13 SETTLEMENT — Jan-May 2026 cumulative ~₹1.97 lakh crore (Business Standard 'Personal Finance' note; March 2026 was largest monthly outflow in 6 years at $13.6bn).** Indian markets bounced 2 consecutive sessions on Trump-Xi summit-pricing (Sensex +0.07% May 13 / +1.0% May 14 morning intraday; Nifty +0.14% / +1.1%) — bounce is summit-pause-pricing, NOT regime reversal; cumulative drawdown from May 11 record still ~-1.83%. **C7 USD/INR ₹95.74 May 14 morning (₹95.31 May 13 close = 4th straight session fresh closing record); pace of weakening MATERIALLY SLOWED from May 11-12 collapse — RBI intervention sustaining defence at ₹95.50-75 zone.** ₹96 trigger Day 2 of 5-7 not breached. **RBI weekly reserves print due ~May 16 (Friday)** = critical signal — if extends above $10bn, intervention sustainability moves from theoretical to operational concern. **Markets dossier C2 reversibility requirements unchanged: (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) USD/INR ₹93 — none triggered.** If Trump leaves Beijing May 15 without deliverable HEU framework, expect today's gains fully reverse Friday-Monday. **Watch Critical: Friday NSDL May 14 FII print — first POSITIVE day would be regime-shift signal**. PRIOR (May 13): **DAY 2 EXTENSION (May 13): FIIs CONTINUED NET SELLERS May 12 (multiple outlets confirm continuation rather than reversal of May 11 ₹8,437 cr outflow pattern); Moody's CUT India FY26 GDP 0.8PP TO 6.0% adds SOVEREIGN-RATING channel to structural FII case; Sensex closed May 12 at 74,559.24 (-1,456 pts, -1.92%) = largest 2-session drawdown of cycle; Hardeep Puri quantified OMC pain at ₹1,000 cr/day = fiscal-deficit channel quantified.** May 13 intraday relief bounce in Nifty/Sensex is technical not fundamental — cumulative drawdown -1.83% from May 11 peak. RBI reserves drawdown next print due ~May 16. FII (Foreign Institutional Investor) net cash-equity flows into Indian listed equities are the **TRANSMISSION MECHANISM** in every India-market causal chain of the cycle. Tracked via NSDL/NSE primary daily prints. **MAY 11 FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — already exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr (+₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12).** Drivers: Trump rejection of Iran counter-proposal + Brent $105.58 + INR breach of ₹95.50 RBI threshold. **The cascade is now self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up (G-Sec 6.96-6.99%) → debt-FPI also out → more INR pressure → more FII exit.** DII absorption (~₹1.7 lakh cr YTD via SIP) covering ~70-90% of FII selling depending on day; gap WIDENING. FPI ownership of Indian equities at ~16%, lowest in nearly two decades. **STRUCTURAL CRACK POINT: if FII outflows accelerate past ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2, DII channel cracks.** Sonal Varma (Nomura): 'Don't expect any sustained pick-up in portfolio flows, as structural concerns over AI-related disruption to India's software industry remain'; recent RBI restrictions on AD forex hedging have increased FX hedge costs, deterring portfolio inflows; sees 'further scope for the rupee to depreciate.'

current.latest_day: FII net -Rs5,556cr (Mon 8 Jun) and ~-Rs4,566cr (Tue 9 Jun) — STILL net-selling both observed sessions; NO confirmed flow turn. DII net +Rs5,165cr (Mon) absorbed ~93% of the FII selling.
current.ytd_cy26_fii_outflow: ~Rs2.5-3.0 lakh cr (sources vary: ~Rs2.54L cr, up to ~Rs2.99L cr) — already exceeds 2025 full-year record
current.capital_rotation: OUT of expensive India INTO the crowded AI/semi trade. The AI trade (leg one) cracked but India FII is STILL net-selling (leg two unmet) — the capital_substitution-reversal thesis is NEGATIVELY tested this window, not advanced. The rotation-to-India remains CONTESTED analyst opinion (Kotak: flows stay muted, India lacks AI exposure / needs an idiosyncratic catalyst; Morgan Stanley GIC: rotate builders->adopters, add EM).
current.dii_buffer: DII net +Rs5,165cr Mon 8 Jun absorbed ~93% of the FII -Rs5,556cr sell — the cleanest single-session proof this cycle that the DII buffer is the proximate support holding the Nifty 23,000 floor (Mon closed 23,123 above the GIFT-indicated gap; index rose Tue/Wed on Trump's tone despite continued FII selling).
current.watch_trigger: WATCHING (markets E1, added 4 Jun): North-Asia AI corrects >10% from peak AND India FII net-positive 3+ sessions = capital_substitution reverses -> India re-rating opens. LEG ONE plausibly advanced; LEG TWO (the FII turn) is NEGATIVELY tested — both observed sessions net-selling. Stays watching.
current.exhaustion_thesis: [UNCHANGED] — the FII-exhaustion / H2-reversal thesis (Outlook Business et al.: ownership below DII, ~90% absorption, 'experts see an H2 reversal') is being recycled VERBATIM; no genuinely new exhaustion datum landed this window — NOT re-presented as new.
current.other_conditions: RBI held 5.25% (neutral). DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor until the FII flow confirms a turn (net-positive 3+/5 consecutive sessions — UNMET).
current.fii_ownership: ~14.7% (April) — ~two-decade low, BELOW DII (~18.9%) for the first time ever
current.dii_absorption: ~93% (Mon 8 Jun: DII +Rs5,165cr vs FII -Rs5,556cr); ~90% on a May run-rate basis (May DII net buying ~Rs82,600 cr)

active_triggers.condition: FII YTD CY26 outflow exceeds ₹2.5 lakh cr by end-Q2 (by 2026-06-30) — DII channel cracks
active_triggers.update_2026-06-06: Headline outflow now ~Rs2.54 lakh crore YTD (Outlook Business, secondary-equities basis) — nominally AT/ABOVE the ₹2.5 lakh cr level. BUT stays WATCHING, not active: the trigger's operative consequence is 'DII channel CRACKS', and the DII channel has NOT cracked — DIIs are still absorbing ~90% of the selling and Nifty is rangebound. Also the ~Rs2.54 lakh cr is a secondary-equities figure whose basis may differ from the trigger's. Threshold flagged as reached; the channel-crack condition is unmet.
active_triggers.note_2026-06-07: 2026-06-07: the Rs2.5L cr OUTFLOW threshold is now REACHED on most tallies (~Rs2.54-2.99L cr) — BUT the trigger's actual cascade ('DII channel cracks') has NOT occurred: DIIs are absorbing ~90% and the Nifty floor held. Outflow limb met; consequence not fired. Stays watching the DII-crack consequence, not the headline number (intel A4).
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: 2026-06-10: the DII channel CONSPICUOUSLY did NOT crack — on Mon 8 Jun, against FII -Rs5,556cr, DIIs bought net +Rs5,165cr (~93% absorption) and held the Nifty 23,000 floor (close 23,123). The outflow limb stays met; the channel-crack consequence is the inverse of fired. STAYS WATCHING the DII-crack consequence, not the headline number (intel A7).

active_triggers.condition: FII flow turn POSITIVE for 5 consecutive sessions — confirms India deployment-zone re-entry
active_triggers.note_2026-06-07: 2026-06-07: STILL UNMET — FII net-selling through Friday; an 'exhaustion/H2-reversal' camp has emerged (ownership below DII; ~90% DII absorption) but no actual flow turn yet. Monday 8 June is the test (intel A4).
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: 2026-06-10: STILL UNMET and now NEGATIVELY tested — both observed sessions were net-selling: FII -Rs5,556cr (Mon 8 Jun), ~-Rs4,566cr (Tue 9 Jun). Not a single net-positive session, let alone 3 consecutive. The 'exhaustion/H2-reversal' camp is [UNCHANGED] (recycled verbatim), not a fresh signal. STAYS WATCHING (intel A7).
active_triggers.note_2026-06-16: 2026-06-16: Not met — last confirmed data was net-selling; no confirmation of a flow turn through the rally. STAYS watching. This + a published signed text remain the two India conviction triggers.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: FII net -Rs5,556cr (Mon 8 Jun) and ~-Rs4,566cr (Tue 9 Jun) — STILL net-selling both observed sessions; the deployment sufficient condition (FII net-positive 3+ sessions) remains UNMET. DII net +Rs5,165cr Mon absorbed ~93% and held the 23,000 floor — the DII buffer is the proximate India support (markets E4). Weight held at 10 — dominant India transmission mechanism, retained per the FII-edge exception.

2026-06-10 (markets E4): the cleanest single-session proof this cycle — DII +Rs5,165cr absorbed ~93% of FII -Rs5,556cr on Mon 8 Jun, holding the Nifty 23,000 floor (close 23,123); the index then ROSE Tue/Wed on Trump's tone despite continued FII selling. The DII buffer is the proximate support. Weight held at 10, retained per the FII-edge exception.

2026-06-10: FII dollar-demand persists (still net-selling); the rupee fell 77p to 95.71 Mon 8 Jun on escalation, then firmed to ~95.40 on Trump's tone — Rs96 pressed but not breached. Weight held at 9, retained per the FII-edge exception.

top_edges.note: bank-nifty is sub-index inside nifty-50 node; edge captures specific bank-financials transmission

recent_signals.headline: No confirmed FII equity-flow turn through the framework rally — last hard data (8-9 Jun) was net-selling (~-Rs5,556cr / -Rs4,566cr, ~93% absorbed by DII). The conviction trigger (5 consecutive net-positive sessions) remains UNMET. Separately, India debt saw ~Rs11,026cr of FAR inflows over 4 days on tax-relief — a non-crisis bond tailwind, not an equity-flow turn. The India deployment trigger (published signed text + FII turn) is not satisfied.

THE UNCONFIRMED DEPLOYMENT LEG. The 12 Jun deal+oil relief rally (Nifty +2.0%) is equity-price action, NOT yet a confirmed FII-flow turn -- the net-positive-3-sessions deployment condition remains the open question and the 12-Jun FII print was not yet available. A one-day rally on deal HOPE is not the institutional-money turn the deployment thesis requires; watch whether FIIs actually buy into the de-escalation or whether DIIs again do the lifting.
sources: markets.md F
Business Standard

recent_signals.headline: FIIs sold ~Rs2,124.98cr Wednesday (10 Jun) -- the net-positive-for-3-sessions deployment condition remains UNMET and not close. May was a RECORD Rs55,963cr FII outflow month, absorbed by DIIs. Leg one of the capital-substitution thesis (North-Asia AI correction >10% -- KOSPI circuit-breaker week, VKOSPI record) is now unambiguously met, but leg two (India FII net-positive) is not: the global AI-trade unwind is hitting India as risk-off, not rotating INTO it.
sources: Business Standard 11 Jun
Republic World (May outflow)
markets.md C4/C6

recent_signals.headline: **THE DII BUFFER HELD THE FLOOR ON CONFIRMED FII SELLING — BUT THE DEPLOYMENT SUFFICIENT CONDITION (FII NET-POSITIVE 3+ SESSIONS) REMAINS UNMET: BOTH OBSERVED SESSIONS NEGATIVE.** FII net -Rs5,556cr (Mon 8 Jun) and ~-Rs4,566cr (Tue 9 Jun); DII net +Rs5,165cr (Mon) absorbed ~93% of the FII selling — the cleanest single-session demonstration this cycle that the DII buffer, not foreign flow, is the proximate support holding the Nifty 23,000 floor (Mon closed 23,123 above the GIFT-indicated gap-down; the index then rose Tue/Wed on Trump's tone DESPITE continued FII selling). The capital_substitution-reversal thesis is NEGATIVELY tested, not advanced: the AI trade (leg one) cracked but India FII is STILL net-selling (leg two unmet). The FII-exhaustion / H2-reversal thesis (Outlook Business et al. — ownership below DII, ~90% absorption, 'experts see an H2 reversal') is being recycled VERBATIM — [UNCHANGED], NOT re-presented as a new development. Triggers: 'FII net-positive 3+/5+ sessions' STAYS WATCHING (unmet, both sessions negative); 'Rs2.5L cr outflow / DII-channel-cracks' STAYS WATCHING (outflow limb long since met, but the DII channel did NOT crack — it absorbed ~93% and held).
sources: Intel 2026-06-10 A7 (FII -Rs5,556cr Mon 8 Jun, ~-Rs4,566cr Tue 9 Jun; DII +Rs5,165cr Mon, ~93% absorption)
Outlook Business et al. (FII-exhaustion / H2-reversal thesis — [UNCHANGED], recycled)
Business Standard / NSE-NSDL flow (Mon 8 Jun close 23,123; rupee 95.71)
recent_signals.verification: FII -Rs5,556cr (Mon 8 Jun) / ~-Rs4,566cr (Tue 9 Jun) + DII +Rs5,165cr Mon (~93% absorption) = CONFIRMED (NSE/NSDL provisional flow, intel A7); DII-buffer-held-23000-floor + index-rose-on-Trump-tone-despite-FII-selling = CONFIRMED (market data); deployment sufficient condition (FII net-positive 3+ sessions) UNMET, both observed sessions negative = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; FII-exhaustion/H2-reversal thesis = [UNCHANGED] (recycled verbatim, NOT a new datum). No trigger moved to active — capital_substitution edge NOT flipped; both FII triggers retain 'watching'.

recent_signals.headline: **FII OWNERSHIP AT A ~TWO-DECADE LOW (~14.7%) — NOW *BELOW* DII (~18.9%) FOR THE FIRST TIME EVER; YTD NET-SELLING ~Rs2.5-3.0L cr, DIIs ABSORBING ~90%; AN 'EXHAUSTION' CAMP EMERGES.** FII ownership of Indian equities has fallen below domestic institutions for the first time in the modern history of Indian capital markets — a structural milestone. YTD CY26 FII net selling (~Rs2.54-2.99L cr on most tallies) already exceeds 2025's full-year record, with DIIs absorbing ~90% (May ~Rs82,600 cr). A new analyst camp argues the selling is nearing EXHAUSTION (H2 reversal) — the marginal foreign seller running low — the bullish counter to Kotak. FII still net-selling through Friday; the deployment-thesis condition (FII net-positive 3+ sessions) is UNMET. Monday 8 June is the real flow test. (CONFIRMED ownership data; REPORTED YTD tallies) (intel A4)
sources: Outlook Business/Maxiom/Republic World (Jun)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (ownership-below-DII milestone); REPORTED (YTD tallies vary by source)

recent_signals.headline: **THE SINK CRACKED HARD — BUT FII IS STILL NET-SELLING, AND THE YTD OUTFLOW NOW EXCEEDS THE ENTIRE 2025 RECORD AT A DECADE-LOW FOREIGN BET.** FIIs have withdrawn ~Rs2.54 lakh crore from Indian secondary equities YTD 2026, already EXCEEDING the ~Rs2.4 lakh crore sold in ALL of 2025 (itself a record) — 'India's foreign-investor bet has shrunk to a DECADE LOW as the AI trade draws capital away' (Outlook Business). DIIs are absorbing ~90% of the FII selling; the DII buffer is now THE transmission mechanism keeping Nifty rangebound (markets E4). The crowded AI/semi trade that has been the SINK for this capital took its biggest crack yet on the 5-June US session (Broadcom AI-guidance miss + hot May jobs 172K vs ~85K -> SMH -9.2%, Nasdaq -4%, VIX +39.7% to ~21.5) — the catalyst this node named for 4+ weeks. BUT leg two of the capital_substitution thesis is NOT confirmed: FII is STILL net-selling, India closed BEFORE the rout (Monday 8 June is the real flow test), and the India-rotation implication is two-camp ANALYST OPINION — Kotak Institutional Equities (FPI flows stay MUTED; India lacks AI-supply-chain exposure, faces its own drags, 'low attractiveness vs other EMs', needs an idiosyncratic catalyst) vs Morgan Stanley GIC (rotate AI 'builders' -> 'adopters', add EM/rest-of-world). The 4-June WATCH trigger advanced on leg one (the >10% correction) but not leg two (the FII turn) — stays WATCHING. SYNTHESIS: necessary condition (AI crack) partially met; sufficient condition (capital choosing India) NOT demonstrated. DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor.
sources: Outlook Business (FII YTD ~Rs2.54 lakh crore withdrawn from secondary equities, exceeds full-2025 record ~Rs2.4 lakh crore; foreign-investor bet at decade low; AI trade draws capital away; DIIs absorb ~90%)
Markets dossier 2026-06-06 (5-June US AI rout: Broadcom miss + May jobs 172K -> SMH -9.2%, Nasdaq -4%, VIX +39.7% ~21.5; DII buffer = proximate support, E4)
Kotak Institutional Equities (FPI flows stay muted; India limited AI exposure; low attractiveness vs other EMs)
Morgan Stanley Global Investment Committee (rotate AI builders -> adopters; add rest-of-world / EM)
recent_signals.verification: FII YTD ~Rs2.54 lakh crore exceeding the full-2025 record (~Rs2.4 lakh crore) + foreign bet at decade low + DII ~90% absorption = REPORTED (Outlook Business); 5-June AI-trade crack (SMH/Nasdaq/VIX) + May jobs 172K = CONFIRMED (market data + BLS); FII-still-net-selling + India-closed-before-rout = CONFIRMED (no flow turn); India-rotation implication = ANALYST OPINION, two camps labeled (Kotak muted vs Morgan Stanley rotate-to-EM) — NOT a confirmed turn. Capital_substitution edge NOT flipped; the 5-consecutive-positive-sessions and ₹2.5-lakh-cr triggers retain 'watching'; WATCH trigger advanced on leg one only. No trigger moved to active.

recent_signals.headline: **THE OUTFLOW DIDN'T SET A NEW RECORD, AND THE AI SINK CRACKED — THE FIRST CONSTRUCTIVE FII SIGNAL IN WEEKS.** The 3 June FII outflow (~Rs5,600cr) was heavy but BELOW the 2 June record (Rs8,362.92cr) — the record was a one-session spike, not a new run-rate. Decisively, the North-Asia AI-memory supercycle that has been the SINK for FII fleeing India took its FIRST valuation crack (KOSPI -5.3%, Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9% on Broadcom's guidance miss, markets C1/E1). For 4+ weeks this node held that India's re-rating requires the FII flow to TURN, which requires winning capital back from the North-Asia AI trade OR that trade cracking — today it cracked. IF Broadcom marks the top, the capital_substitution edge can fire in REVERSE (AI sell -> India bid), the cleanest forward catalyst for the India thesis. A WATCH trigger was added on nifty-50 (E1): North-Asia AI corrects >10% from peak AND India FII turns net-positive 3+ sessions = substitution reverses. NOT a confirmed turn — one session is not a reversal; the binding condition (FII positive 5 consecutive sessions) is unconfirmed, and a violent AI crack pulls capital OUT of EM India first. RBI held 5.25%, oil softened sub-$95, rupee firmed sub-Rs96 — the other reversibility conditions held; DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-06-04 C1/C5/E1 (AI-memory crack = first session capital_substitution could reverse; WATCH trigger; do NOT flip sign)
Intel 2026-06-04 A7 (3 Jun FII ~Rs5,600cr, below 2 Jun record), A6 (RBI hold)
recent_signals.verification: 3 Jun FII ~Rs5,600cr below 2 Jun record = REPORTED (provisional intraday tallies vary by provider); AI-memory crack tape = CONFIRMED (KOSPI/Samsung/SK Hynix market data); capital_substitution potential reversal = STRUCTURAL ANALYSIS (markets E1), NOT a confirmed turn; the 5-consecutive-positive-sessions trigger retains 'watching' status — no flow turn confirmed. No trigger moved to active.

recent_signals.headline: **FII/PASSIVE FLOWS THE TRANSMISSION MECHANISM IN EVERY INDIA CHAIN — NEW MECHANICAL LEG (MSCI REBALANCE).** The 29 May Nifty -1.5% drop on a relief day was driven by the MSCI rebalance ($800m-$1bn one-time passive outflow; Hyundai India -$281m, Jubilant -$161m steepest) + discretionary selling + structural rotation out of expensive India (~24x P/E) into the Korea/Taiwan AI supercycle (India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%; Goldman India marketweight / Korea highest-conviction +300% EPS). War-period cumulative outflows >$22bn; FY26 net FPI ~-$16.7bn. This is why the relief leg fails.
sources: BusinessUpturn (MSCI $800m-$1bn); Goldman (paired call); wire
Intel 2026-05-30 A5; Market C2, C3, E1
recent_signals.verification: MSCI $800m-$1bn = REPORTED (wire); rotation + Goldman paired call = CONFIRMED (research + flow data); cumulative outflows = CONFIRMED. The dominant explanation for India underperformance.

recent_signals.headline: **FY26 FII OUTFLOWS NOW ~₹3.33 LAKH CRORE — THE CAPITAL IS ROTATING OUT OF INDIA INTO THE KOREA/TAIWAN AI TRADE, AND OIL RELIEF CAN'T REVERSE IT.** Day 90: the FII channel stayed the binding negative in every India chain. FY26 cumulative outflows ~₹3.33 lakh crore (Republic World May 28), against 15-year-low FPI ownership (~16%, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). **The decisive read**: this is not merely crisis de-risking — foreign money is rotating OUT of India INTO the Korea/Taiwan AI trade (**India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%**), so ~40% of the India weakness is a valuation-rotation that does NOT reverse on a deal, while ~60% is the crisis component that does. **Why oil relief can't fix it**: India printed a second straight down session despite operative Brent sub-$100 — oil-import relief is necessary but not sufficient when the marginal seller is structurally exiting an expensive (~24x P/E), FII-dependent market for a cheaper, faster-growing AI-semis EM. The RBI's deep macroprudential toolbox can cap the rupee but cannot make FIIs net buyers — relief is paper, positioning is structural. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a) but (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed and FY26 outflows are still climbing; DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-28 C5 (capital rotation OUT of India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI trade; ~60% crisis / ~40% valuation-rotation; India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42%; oil relief necessary not sufficient)
Intel dossier 2026-05-28 A5 (FII/FPI structural backdrop)
Republic World May 28 (FY26 FII cumulative outflows ~₹3.33 lakh crore); Business Standard May 28 (India second down session on foreign-fund outflows)
recent_signals.verification: FY26 FII cumulative outflows ~₹3.33 lakh crore = REPORTED (Republic World May 28); FPI ~16% / DII>FII in Nifty 500 (DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1%) = CONFIRMED structural reading; rotation OUT of India INTO Korea/Taiwan AI trade + India -9% YTD vs Korea +90% / Taiwan +42% = CONFIRMED relative-performance data + analytical read (markets dossier C5); ~60% crisis / ~40% valuation-rotation + oil-relief-necessary-not-sufficient = CONFIRMED analytical read; second-down-session-despite-sub-$100-oil = CONFIRMED (Business Standard). NOTE: the india↔semiconductor capital-rotation edge is being added to edges.json by the lead engineer — referenced here, NOT edited. No trigger moved to active — the ₹2.5 lakh cr / 5-session-positive triggers retain prior watching status.

recent_signals.headline: **THE RELIEF SESSIONS DID NOT TURN THE FII CHANNEL — THE BOUNCE WAS PAPER, NOT POSITIONING, AND IT REVERSED ON THE STRIKES.** Over the ~48h window (no brief May 26), FII flow remained the binding negative in every India chain: record FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore YTD 2026 (Republic World; already exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-yr low, below DII — DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 = structural rotation, not just flow). The May 25 deal-relief bounce (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22) reversed when US CONFIRMED self-defense strikes inside southern Iran 'dampened hopes for a peace deal' — on Tue May 26 India gave back the rally (Nifty -0.49% to 23,913.70) as **FII outflows weighed and only DII inflows cushioned**; the Nifty MidCap still printed a fresh high (+0.54%) purely on DII absorption — the cleanest demonstration that the floor is domestic, not foreign. **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: oil-relief + the RBI $5B swap (~1.96x cover, healthy bank dollar-appetite, not stress) + the May 25 'undervalued' jawboning can cap the currency and bid the index, but they CANNOT make FIIs net buyers — relief is paper, positioning is structural. **Reversibility requires the FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Operative Brent ~$99 is near (a), but FII and INR both moved the WRONG way this window; (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed. The markets dossier (E4) promotes fii-flows as THE recurring transmission mechanism and is explicit that the crisis component reverses fast on a SIGNED deal while the FII/structural component does not.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-27 C2 (India relief bounce that didn't come; FII outflows weighed, DII cushioned), F (FII record -₹1.92 lakh cr YTD), E4 (promote fii-flows as recurring transmission mechanism)
Intel dossier 2026-05-27 B1 (India gave back Monday's rally on FII outflows; DII inflows + MidCap fresh high), A1 (US strikes CONFIRMED ACTION), A7 (RBI swap 1.96x cover)
Republic World (FII -₹1.92 lakh cr YTD 2026 record); Business Standard May 26 'US strikes roil Iran peace hopes'
recent_signals.verification: FII record outflow ₹1.92 lakh cr YTD 2026 = CONFIRMED (Republic World, markets dossier F); FPI ~16% / DII>FII in Nifty 500 = CONFIRMED structural reading; May 26 reversal with FII outflows weighing + DII cushioning + MidCap fresh high = CONFIRMED (intel B1 + Business Standard); 'paper not positioning' + FII-the-binding-reversibility-condition = CONFIRMED analytical read (markets dossier C2/E4); RBI swap 1.96x cover (caps currency, cannot turn positioning) = CONFIRMED (intel A7).

recent_signals.headline: **DEAL-RELIEF SESSION (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22) BID THE INDEX BUT DID NOT TURN THE FII CHANNEL — THE BOUNCE IS PAPER, NOT POSITIONING.** With US/UK shut for holiday, India's domestic-only session caught the deal-relief leg (Brent <$100 + US-Iran de-escalation hopes + RBI 'INR undervalued' jawboning), Nifty +1.32% to 24,031.70, USD/INR firmed to ₹95.22, India VIX -7.9%. **But the structural FII overhang is unchanged**: YTD CY26 outflow still tracking >₹2.19 lakh crore (exceeds full-year 2025's ₹1.66 lakh crore), FPI ownership ~16% (15-year low; DII 20.9% vs FII 17.1% in Nifty 500 — structural rotation, not just flow), and DII SIP-led buying remains the only floor. **The mechanism / decision-relevant read**: a single risk-on session lifts the index on paper but does not reverse foreign positioning — RBI intervention/jawboning + oil-relief can cap the currency and bid the tape, but cannot make FIIs net buyers. The markets dossier is explicit that ~75% of today's Nifty gain reverses on deal-failure + oil re-spike, and the FII flows / cumulative fuel pass-through / RBI reserve drawdown that hold the structural bear are all unchanged. **Reversibility requires FII flow to turn positive** — (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII flow turn positive AND (c) organic USD/INR ₹93-94. Monday moved (a) and (c) marginally right; (b), the binding condition, is unconfirmed. Tuesday May 26 ($5B RBI swap auction + Quad) is the next institutional read.
sources: Markets dossier 2026-05-25 Section C2 (relief-bounce-not-re-rating; FII/fuel/reserve overhang unchanged)
Business Standard May 25 (Nifty +1.32%, USD/INR ₹95.22, RBI 'INR undervalued')
Intel dossier 2026-05-25 A10, A11
recent_signals.verification: Nifty +1.32% / USD/INR ₹95.22 / VIX -7.9% relief session = CONFIRMED market data (markets dossier Section A + Business Standard); FII structural overhang unchanged (YTD >₹2.19 lakh cr, FPI ~16%, DII>FII) = CONFIRMED carried structural data (no new positive-flow print today); 'paper not positioning' + ~75% reversal-on-failure = CONFIRMED analytical read (markets dossier C2).

recent_signals.headline: **FII OUTFLOWS YTD ₹2.2 TRILLION — ALREADY EXCEED ENTIRE 2025 (₹1.66 trillion); DII OWNERSHIP NOW SURPASSES FII (20.9% vs 17.1% IN NIFTY 500).** Through first week of May, FPIs withdrew ₹1.92 lakh crore from Indian equities (Republic World); April alone saw ₹60,847 crore outflow. **DII ownership in Nifty 500 now 20.9%, surpassing FII's 17.1% — structural rotation, not just flow.** The DII absorption of 'nearly 90% of outflows through strong SIP-led buying' is the technical reason Indian indices haven't capitulated despite the structural bear. **The Sensex/Nifty +0.4%/+0.3% relief rally is misdirection** — IT exporter names (TCS, Infosys, HCL +3-4.5% on weaker rupee) catch the postponement-relief while the underlying economy worsens. **None of the three Indian deployment conditions (Brent <$95, FII flows net positive, INR retracement to ₹93) is met. All three moving the wrong direction.**
sources: Republic World (FII outflow ₹1.92 lakh crore through first week May)
Business Standard (Rs 27,048 crore MTD May)
Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A13; Markets dossier C6
recent_signals.verification: FII outflows YTD ₹2.2 trillion = CONFIRMED (Republic World + Business Standard); DII >FII in Nifty 500 = CONFIRMED structural reading; SIP-led DII absorption ~90% of outflows = REPORTED ongoing structural pattern.

recent_signals.headline: FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr MAY 11 = highest since April 24. DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70% (₹5,939.65 cr). YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR — exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr) by ₹40k cr; added ₹14k cr in just 2 sessions May 11-12. Drivers: Trump rejection 'TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE' + Brent $105.58 + INR ₹95.54 fresh record breaking RBI ₹95.50 threshold + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes. Cascade self-reinforcing: weaker rupee → imported inflation → bond yields up → debt-FPI out → more INR pressure. RBI forex reserves -$7.79bn week ended May 1 to $690.69bn = evidence of active intervention not stopping the move. Sonal Varma (Nomura) flagging 'further depreciation' on AI-disruption + RBI hedging restrictions deterring inflows.
sources: NSDL FPI data (primary)
NSE official daily flow
Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs
Republic World / Outlook Money (₹2.06 lakh cr tally)
Nomura Sonal Varma research
